channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Pakistani security forces battle to free about 300 hostages aboard a hijacked train
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani security forces battled hundreds of separatist militants who were holding about 300 people hostage Wednesday on a train they hijacked in the country’s remote southwest, officials said. Militants wearing vests loaded with explosives surrounded the hostages.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports go into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump officially increased tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports to 25% on Wednesday, promising that the taxes would help create U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NOAA set to fire 1,029 more employees
item.description: Staffing levels at the nation's already hobbled weather, climate and oceans agency could take a further nosedive in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KTLA 5

item.title: Manhunt underway for Philly driver who opened fire on teen, woman in road rage incident
item.description: A manhunt is underway for a driver accused of shooting a 15-year-old boy and a 20-year-old woman after an apparent road rage incident in West Philadelphia, police say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels say 'any Israeli vessel' in nearby Mideast waterways again a target
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels warned shippers early Wednesday that “any Israeli vessel” traveling through nearby Mideast waters is now a target as Israel continues to block aid to the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Court stays execution of Texas man days before he was set to die by lethal injection
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas appeals court on Tuesday halted the execution of a man who has spent more than 30 years on death row and had been set to die by lethal injection this week over the killings of six girls and young women found buried in the desert near El Paso.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Harvey Weinstein due in court for key rulings as his #MeToo retrial nears
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Harvey Weinstein 's #MeToo retrial next month will largely be an abridged version of the original, with one big addition: a charge based on an allegation from a woman who wasn’t a part of the first case.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Most AAPI adults don't support cutting agencies and want a focus on costs: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: A new AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll shows that most Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders do not agree with the strategy of putting entire federal agencies on the chopping block, nor are they broadly on board with mass layoffs of federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Terry Tang And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Most AAPI adults don't support cutting agencies and want a focus on costs: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders do not agree with the strategy of putting entire federal agencies on the chopping block, nor are they broadly on board with mass layoffs of federal workers, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil didn't wear a mask. He's now the face of Trump's crackdown on campus protests
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When protests over the Israel-Hamas war took root on Columbia University's campus last spring, Mahmoud Khalil became a familiar, outspoken figure in a student movement that soon spread to other U.S. colleges.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration halts $1 billion program that keeps aging affordable housing livable
item.description: The Associated Press has obtained a document showing that the Trump administration is halting a $1 billion program that helps preserve affordable housing.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: States should scale back abortion reporting demands, advocacy group says
item.description: States should stop requiring health providers to file reports on every abortion because the information poses a risk to both them and their patients in the current political environment, a research group that advocates for abortion access says.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrat David Gottfried easily wins Minnesota House special election, restoring a 67-67 power split
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Voters in a Minnesota House district at the center of post-election drama over chamber control decisively chose Democrat David Gottfried on Tuesday, tying the House and ending a short-lived Republican majority.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Infowars employee killed in Texas, law enforcement confirms
item.description: Jamie White, an Infowars employee, was the victim of a homicide in southeast Austin, Texas, late Sunday night, law enforcement sources confirmed to Nexstar's KXAN.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Hollis, WGN 9

item.title: Justice Department is expected to slash public corruption unit, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prosecutors in the Justice Department section that handles public corruption cases have been told the unit will be significantly reduced in size, and its cases will be transferred to U.S. attorney's offices around the country, two people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump halts doubling of tariffs on Canadian metals after Ontario suspends electricity price hikes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's threat Tuesday to double his planned tariffs on steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% for Canada led the provincial government of Ontario to suspend its planned surcharges on electricity sold to the United States.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: One cat's incredible reunion with its owner after the LA wildfires
item.description: It appears the tall tale that all cats have nine lives may be true for a California Maine coon named Aggie. The beloved feline was feared dead for two months after the Palisades wildfire in Los Angeles left her family's home in ashes. But her owner, 82-year-old Katherine Kiefer, held out some hope.
item.dc:creator: BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Garlin Gilchrist announces Democratic bid to become Michigan's first Black governor
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Garlin Gilchrist II, a Democrat from Detroit who is Michigan's first Black lieutenant governor, announced he's jumping into the crowded 2026 race for governor on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans warn Trump not to let trade war drag as he doubles down on tariff threats
item.description: Senate Republicans are warning President Trump to not let his trade war drag on as his latest escalation sent Wall Street further into turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: White House pressures Columbia University as it seeks to deport pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The White House complained Tuesday that Columbia University is refusing to help federal agents find people being sought as part of the government’s effort to deport participants in pro-Palestinian demonstrations, as the administration continued to punish the school by yanking federal
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHS ending collective bargaining for transportation security officers
item.description: "They gave as a justification a completely fabricated claim about union officials," AFGE National President Everett Kelley said.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Department of Education lays off nearly half of workforce
item.description: The Department of Education announced Tuesday it was firing nearly half of its workforce, the latest Trump administration move to shrink the federal government that could face swift legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about Mahmoud Khalil, the Columbia protester arrested by ICE and facing deportation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Palestinian activist who helped lead student protests at Columbia University faces deportation after being arrested over the weekend by federal immigration officials.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing woman survives nearly a week after Northwest Indiana crash
item.description: Brieonna Cassell, 41, of Wheatfield, was reported missing last Thursday, according to the Newton County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he’s buying ‘brand new’ Tesla to support Musk
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would be buying a “brand new” Tesla to support his senior adviser Elon Musk, as the stock market shows shares of the electric car company tumbling.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Dems accused of threatening government shutdown over Musk hatred and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Medical helicopter crash in Mississippi kills 3
item.description: MADISON COUNTY, Miss. (AP) — A medical transport helicopter crashed in Mississippi on Monday, killing a pilot and two hospital workers on board, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Social Security to again recoup 100% of overpayments
item.description: The Office of the Chief Actuary estimated the withholding rate increase could save about $7 billion over the next decade.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Arrest of Palestinian activist stirs questions about protections for students and green card holders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The arrest of a Palestinian activist who helped organize campus protests of the war in Gaza has sparked questions about whether foreign students and green card holders are protected against being deported from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LA district attorney says he won’t support resentencing the Menendez brothers because they lied
item.description: Resentencing, which could make the brothers immediately eligible for parole, is just one of the pathways they are pursuing to walk free after their 1996 convictions for murder.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Stocks plunge as Wall Street wonders how much economic pain Trump will accept
item.description: The economy has already given some signals of weakening, mostly through surveys showing increased pessimism.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Greenland votes amid Trump invitation to join US: What to know
item.description: Greenland's upcoming national election could set the stage for a later referendum on its future control, as President Trump has renewed his quest to take over the natural resources-rich arctic land.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KRON4

item.title: Measles case confirmed in Maryland resident who recently travelled abroad
item.description: Early symptoms include a fever higher than 101 degrees, a runny nose, cough and red, watery eyes. A person will experience a red rash one to four days after early symptoms.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: New York governor declares state of emergency for wind-driven brush fire on Long Island
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Fast-moving brush fires fanned by high winds burned through a large swath of land on New York's Long Island on Saturday, spewing thick gray smoke into the sky and prompting the evacuation of a military base and the closure of a major highway. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy arrested for assault in Texas
item.description: On Friday, Chiefs star rookie Xavier Worthy, 21, was arrested in Williamson County, Texas after investigators said he assaulted a family member or person in his household by "impeding their breathing or circulation.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, WFLA, NBC
